title: Baklava Walnut Honey Layers

seo.title: Walnut Baklava Recipe: Authentic Honey Layers
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Walnut Baklava! This easy recipe features flaky phyllo, sweet honey syrup, and crunchy walnuts for a perfect Middle Eastern dessert.
seo.h1: Authentic Walnut Baklava Recipe with Honey Layers
h2Headings: Step-by-Step Guide to Making Walnut Baklava
seo.imageAlt: A tray of golden, glistening authentic walnut baklava cut into diamonds, soaked in honey syrup.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, sweet, and nutty flavors of homemade authentic Walnut Baklava, a classic Middle Eastern dessert.

description: Baklava Walnut Honey Layers is a revered Middle Eastern dessert, celebrated for its exquisite balance of textures and flavors. Delicate layers of flaky phyllo dough are generously filled with finely chopped walnuts, then baked to a golden crisp perfection. Finally, the warm pastry is drenched in a fragrant, sweet honey syrup, creating an irresistible treat that melts in your mouth.

instructions.title: Prepare the Walnut Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the finely chopped walnuts, ground cinnamon, optional cardamom, and optional cloves. Mix well and set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Honey Syrup
instructions.text: In a saucepan, combine the granulated sugar, water, and honey. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes until slightly thickened. Stir in the lemon juice, then remove from heat and let it cool completely.

instructions.title: Assemble the First Layers
instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350F (175C). Brush a 9x13 inch baking pan with melted butter. Lay a sheet of phyllo dough in the pan, brush lightly with melted butter. Repeat this process for about 8-10 layers, ensuring each sheet is buttered.

instructions.title: Add Walnut Layers
instructions.text: Sprinkle about one-third of the walnut mixture evenly over the buttered phyllo layers. Place 2-3 more phyllo sheets on top, buttering each one. Add another third of the walnut mixture. Repeat with 2-3 more buttered phyllo sheets and the remaining walnuts.

instructions.title: Finish the Top Layers
instructions.text: Cover the remaining walnuts with the rest of the phyllo sheets, buttering each layer generously. Once all phyllo is used, brush the very top layer with butter. Using a sharp knife, carefully score the baklava into diamond or square shapes, cutting all the way through to the bottom.

instructions.title: Bake and Pour Syrup
instructions.text: Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown and puffed. Remove from oven and immediately and slowly pour the cooled honey syrup evenly over the hot baklava. The sizzle means it is working! Allow baklava to cool completely for several hours or overnight to absorb the syrup.

instructions.title: Serve and Enjoy
instructions.text: Once completely cooled and the syrup has been fully absorbed, cut along the scored lines to separate the pieces. Garnish with additional chopped walnuts or pistachios if desired. Serve at room temperature.

tips: Keep phyllo dough covered with a damp cloth while working to prevent drying out. This ensures your layers remain pliable and dont crack.
For an extra fragrant syrup, add a cinnamon stick, a few whole cloves, or a strip of orange peel to the syrup mixture while simmering.
Store baklava at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks. Do not refrigerate as the moisture can make the phyllo soggy.
article: Baklava Walnut Honey Layers is a beloved Middle Eastern Desserts dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
